sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: CD Nacional have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, securing 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, with a notable struggle in defense conceding 8 goals. FC Arouca are also in poor form, with no wins in their last 4 matches (1 win, 2 draws, 2 losses), conceding heavily (13 goals in 5 games). Both teams are showing vulnerabilities, especially Arouca with no clean sheets in 9 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, FC Arouca have dominated with 2 wins (1-0 and 2-1), scoring early in both games. Arouca have been the first to score in 7 of their last 8 encounters against Nacional, indicating a trend of early control.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: CD Nacional struggle with possession (38.6%, league rank 18) and have created few big chances (5, rank 17), though they convert at a decent rate (40%). FC Arouca also share similar stats with poor possession and defensive issues (13 goals conceded, among the worst in the league). Both teams show high foul counts, suggesting a physical game.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends
sections.text: Matches involving Nacional often see under 2.5 goals (4/5 recent games), while Arouca's games tend to have fewer corners (under 10.5 in 4/5). Head-to-head games frequently exceed 4.5 cards and 10.5 corners, pointing to an intense, competitive clash.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, CD Nacional and FC Arouca are locked in a goalless draw at Estádio da Madeira. Despite the 0-0 scoreline, the first half was far from uneventful, with CD Nacional dominating much of the play. The home side controlled possession and created several promising opportunities, while FC Arouca struggled to make an impact in the attacking third. Both teams will be looking to capitalize on their chances in the second half, but Nacional appears to have the upper hand based on the opening 45 minutes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance: CD Nacional
halftimeSections.text: CD Nacional has been the more assertive side in the first half, enjoying a significant share of possession and generating multiple chances to score. They created big opportunities but were unable to convert, missing key moments in front of goal. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area underline their attacking intent. Defensively, they’ve been solid, winning a majority of duels and showing resilience against Arouca’s limited threats. Players like Pablo Ruan have been influential in creating chances, while Jesús Ramírez came close to breaking the deadlock.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance: FC Arouca
halftimeSections.text: FC Arouca, on the other hand, has had a tough first half, unable to impose themselves on the game. Their attacking output has been minimal, with no significant chances created and a lack of presence in the opposition’s penalty area. They’ve been outplayed in possession and duels, often finding themselves on the back foot. Defensively, they’ve managed to hold firm, thanks in part to goalkeeper João Valido’s strong performance, but their overall lack of threat suggests they need a major shift in approach after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung heavily in favor of CD Nacional for the majority of the period. They started to assert control early on with consistent attacking spells, particularly in the middle stages of the half, where they maintained pressure for extended periods. FC Arouca had brief moments of momentum, especially in the opening minutes and sporadically towards the end of the half, but these were fleeting and lacked sustained threat. Nacional’s persistent forward push indicates they are more likely to break through if the trend continues.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, CD Nacional seems poised to continue their dominance unless FC Arouca can regroup and offer more in attack. Nacional’s ability to create chances and their higher expected goals suggest they are the favorites to find the net. Arouca will need to improve their final third entries and capitalize on any rare opportunities to turn the tide. Tactical adjustments, especially from the away side, could play a crucial role, but Nacional’s home advantage and first-half performance give them the edge.
